beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning wake-up with full energy; sunny weather sets a positive tone for the day.

beats.activity: Commute by car to Bellevue workplace.
beats.reasoning: West Seattle to Bellevue is a 35–40 min drive; Light Rail closure won't affect her car commute. Leaving early to settle in before work.

beats.activity: Morning work session at cloud infrastructure startup.
beats.reasoning: Settling into work routine; stopping at 11am to watch the USA vs Wales FIFA match, which airs live and is a notable event she won't skip.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match at a downtown bar with colleagues.
beats.reasoning: Bars downtown open early for the 11am match; Zoe joins coworkers for the game—a rare social outing that fits the sunny Monday energy and doesn't conflict with her homebody nature (work friends, low-key setting).

beats.activity: Lunch at a casual spot near the bar.
beats.reasoning: Still in downtown; staying close to coworkers, light meal after the match before heading back to the office.
Return to work after the match break; focused afternoon push before commute home.
40-min drive from Bellevue back to West Seattle; Light Rail closure ended at 3pm, but driving home is her normal routine.

beats.activity: Pick up groceries and run a quick errand.
beats.reasoning: Home neighborhood errand; sunny evening is ideal for a quick stop before settling in; fits her community-minded, planned approach to daily tasks.
Homebody settling in for the evening; cooking a simple meal from her groceries.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather and decided to drive in—Light Rail was down anyway. The morning blur of coffee and commute felt routine until the team suggested hitting that bar downtown for the World Cup match. I'm not huge on soccer, but watching USA vs Wales with everyone cheering was surprisingly fun. The energy downtown was electric, all those early-opening bars packed with fans. We grabbed lunch after, riding that buzz back to the office. Spent the afternoon pushing through some infrastructure configs—felt productive, maybe because I was still riding the social high. Drive home was smooth, grabbed groceries at Safeway, and threw together a simple dinner. By the time I hit my pillow, I felt that good kind of tired. Feels nice when a Monday doesn't feel like a Monday.
thought_process: I almost stayed in and worked through lunch, but the World Cup thing caught me off-guard—something about the spontaneity appealed to me. The maintenance closure actually worked in my favor since I'd driven anyway. The bar outing shifted my whole mood; I went from autopilot-Zoe to present-Zoe. It reminded me I actually enjoy my team outside of Slack. Coming back to work felt easier, less like grinding. Even the commute home felt lighter. I think I needed that social break more than I realized. The sunny weather probably helped too—everything felt more possible today.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
